scope.target: Executable governed version-to-version migration metadata and extracted-package upgrade validation
constraints: Bind immutable previous and incoming inventory identities
Stop for SemVer reclassification before adding a schema or new public contract

steps: Reproduce the v0.4.0 clean-install-only metadata contradiction
Design the smallest patch-compatible previous-inventory input
Add clean-install and real v0.3.0-to-v0.4.1 extracted-package fixtures
Build twice from an immutable candidate ref and compare archives
validation: Focused package builder and apply GWT
Real extracted v0.3.0 to candidate upgrade
Local modification, removal, rename, and reconciliation scenarios
execution.last_completed_step: Built and applied a real candidate archive from the immutable v0.3.0 package inventory.
execution.next_action: Continue with V041-003 and keep source-only release and builder tests out of the downstream gate.

results.summary: The existing migration schema now emits executable versioned operations when the builder receives the exact governed v0.3.0 files manifest; clean install remains supported separately.
tests_run: python .ai/scripts/tests/test_ai_context_package_apply.py -v (13 passed, 1 Windows symlink privilege skip)
real v0.3.0 extracted payload to v0.4.1 candidate dry-run and apply
ZIP/tar.gz member parity and checksum sidecars
results.residual_risk: Migration schema 1.0.0 supports one automatic source; multi-source direct upgrades are explicitly deferred to v0.5.0.
